build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors.

requirements.description: The player moves in a top-down view with WASD, aims with mouse, and attacks with click. Both player and enemies die in one hit, creating instant-death tension. Score 0 if there is no movement or combat. Score 0.5 at most if movement exists but there is no one-hit-kill mechanic for both sides. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A combo scoring system rewards chaining kills without pause — the multiplier increases with each rapid kill and decays or resets if the player hesitates. Score 0 if no combo system exists. Score 0.5 at most if a score exists but has no time-based multiplier mechanic. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Doors can be interacted with (kicked open) to stun enemies behind them, and weapons can be picked up from the ground with limited ammo or single use. Score 0 if there are no interactive environment elements or weapon pickups. Score 0.5 at most if doors exist but cannot stun, or weapons exist but have infinite ammo. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Death instantly restarts the current floor with no loading screen, maintaining the die-and-retry flow. Score 0 if death ends the game or requires navigating menus to retry. Score 0.5 at most if restart exists but has a long delay or loading interruption. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Enemies patrol set routes with visible awareness states; alerted enemies actively pursue and attack the player. Score 0 if enemies are static or have no AI behavior. Score 0.5 at most if enemies move but have no patrol/alert state distinction. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least 8 distinct floor layouts with different room configurations, corridor arrangements, and enemy placements. Score 0 if only one floor exists. Score 0.5 at most if floors differ only in enemy count with identical layouts. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 5 distinct weapon types with different behaviors (melee, pistol, shotgun, SMG, thrown) that force different tactical approaches. Score 0 if only one weapon exists. Score 0.5 at most if weapons differ only in damage with identical behavior. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 3 distinct enemy types with different behaviors (stationary guard, patrolling, armored, fast rusher) requiring different engagement strategies. Score 0 if only one enemy type exists. Score 0.5 at most if enemies look different but behave identically. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Floor-clear scoring shows time, combo chain, weapon variety bonus, and overall grade with visible ranking. Score 0 if no scoring exists. Score 0.5 at most if only a single number is shown with no breakdown. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A campaign progression system tracks completed floors, best scores, and unlocks access to later floors. Score 0 if all floors are available from the start with no progression. Score 0.5 at most if progression exists but has no visible tracking or unlock feedback. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Normal startup shows a styled title screen with the game name, floor selection, and action-themed imagery. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse text on a blank background. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The flow connects title to floor selection to gameplay to score screen without restarting the executable. Score 0 if major transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if transitions exist but break visually. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The HUD shows current combo multiplier, weapon and ammo count, and enemy count remaining in readable non-overlapping regions. Score 0 if the player cannot determine game state. Score 0.5 at most if information is present but overlapping or unreadable. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Player actions produce visible feedback: attacks show impact, kills spray effects, doors kicked show stun, combo increases flash, and death triggers a distinct animation. Score 0 if actions happen silently. Score 0.5 at most if only one feedback type exists. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Art direction is coherent across title, floors, enemies, weapons, HUD, and score screens with a consistent gritty action palette and style. Score 0 if mostly default controls or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

The player character, enemies, weapons, doors, and floor tiles use real sprite art with distinct designs. Score 0 if primary gameplay elements are unstyled rectangles. Score 0.5 at most if sprites exist but are inconsistent in style. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation brings floors to life: enemy patrol animations, attack effects, door interactions, death sequences, and combo visual escalation. Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if only one element animates. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI panels use styled themed widgets with custom fonts, framed panels, or action-styled elements rather than raw Godot defaults. Score 0 if all UI is default Label/Button. Score 0.5 at most if some panels are styled but others remain raw defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
